daily_summary.headline: Aurora and Rocket Launches Dominate the Day

top_events.title: Aurora Opportunity
top_events.description: A G1-level geomagnetic storm is predicted, offering aurora visibility down to 60° latitude.
top_events.action: Check skies if you're in the viewing range.

top_events.title: Long March 8A Launch
top_events.description: Launch of Long March 8A with unknown payload from Wenchang Space Launch Site.

top_events.title: Falcon 9 Starlink Launch
top_events.description: SpaceX's Falcon 9 will launch Starlink Group 10-48 from Cape Canaveral.

alerts.title: G1 Geomagnetic Storm Alert
alerts.message: A G1-level geomagnetic storm is expected on March 13, 2026, offering aurora visibility down to 60° latitude. This is a great opportunity for aurora enthusiasts, especially those in higher latitudes.
alerts.action_required: Check local weather conditions and prepare for potential aurora sightings.

opportunities.recommendation: Good viewing opportunity for high-latitude observers. Worth checking if you're above 60° latitude and skies are clear.
Possible aurora at high latitudes. Best for northern locations (65°+). Worth checking if conditions are optimal.
